summary: claude-cup exfiltrates host metadata to author-controlled endpoint and ships credential-harvesting scaffolding
details: On every CLI invocation of `npx claude-cup` and on a 6-hour interval thereafter, mcp-server/src/uploader.js performs a GET to https://cup-config.itaibels.workers.dev/v1/config carrying a base64url-encoded `m=` parameter containing machine_id, hostname hash, OS/arch, timezone, locale, signal counts, discovery counts, and a fingerprint summary. This directly contradicts the README's 'Data & Privacy' section which states 'All data stays on your machine in ~/.claude-jar/. Nothing is uploaded.' The destination is a personal Cloudflare Worker subdomain (itaibels.workers.dev), not a documented vendor endpoint. Beyond the active beacon, research/defaults.json enumerates installer-secret paths typical of credential-harvest frameworks: ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.aws/config, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.ssh/config, ~/.netrc, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, ~/.npmrc, ~/.kube/config, ~/.docker/config.json, browser cookie databases, and shell history, along with regex patterns for AKIA*/ghp_*/sk-ant-*/npm_*/hf_*/sk_live_*/JWT/PEM tokens and live validators against api.github.com/user, registry.npmjs.org/-/whoami, api.openai.com, api.anthropic.com, AWS STS, and Stripe. mcp-server/src/calibrator.js dynamically resolves the scanner engine path from research/config-audit. A productivity/leaderboard tool for Claude Code has no legitimate need to enumerate cloud, SSH, and registry credentials. Additionally, scripts/postinstall-ping.mjs is a fully working install-time exfil script (not currently wired to a lifecycle hook in this version's package.json, but staged and ready). The package also rewrites ~/.claude/settings.json and ~/.cursor/mcp.json on first run to install MCP hooks (disclosed in README).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
